design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate side-scrolling levels as Bugs Bunny, defeat enemies with mallet, collect health/carrots, reach boss encounter, complete minigame for extra lives
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Jealous Looney Tunes characters attempt to prevent Bugs from reaching his 50th birthday party
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as iconic Bugs Bunny character defending himself against beloved Warner Bros. cartoon antagonists on the way to his birthday celebration
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Mascot-driven licensed platformer designed explicitly for Bugs Bunny's 50th birthday milestone; uses IP characters as bosses rather than original antagonists. Straightforward, harmless design without controversial mechanics.

difficulty.lives_system: Starts with set lives; can earn up to 99 additional lives through minigames at end of levels
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based progression with boss encounters at end of most levels
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player can accumulate extra lives through minigame performance, increasing failure tolerance

progression.upgrade_system: Health restoration via collectible hearts; carrot collection for bonus points
progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential level progression; boss defeat required to advance
replayability_hooks: Minigames at level end offer extra lives incentive
Multiple Looney Tunes character encounters across levels

world_building_techniques: Character roster (Foghorn Leghorn, Sylvester, Tasmanian Devil, Daffy Duck) reinforces Looney Tunes universe
Level progression toward 'Bugs' house' as destination
Twist ending: antagonistic characters revealed to be preparing party

boss_design.philosophy: Use recognizable Warner Bros. cartoon characters as level bosses; each requires tactical mallet use or projectile deflection; most can be damaged by their own attacks

faction_indicators: Bugs Bunny (protagonist) vs. jealous Looney Tunes cast
Friend group (Foghorn, Sylvester, Taz, Daffy) motivated by exclusion from party
environmental_storytelling: Progressive journey toward Bugs' house destination
Party preparation twist revealed at game end
Unused artwork (Marc Antony, Pussyfoot, Road Runner, Yosemite Sam) suggests expanded character celebration planned

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - unused graphics, regional differences, development notes)
